free_text: Radiative capture, particle emission and fission in
heavy nuclei
External beam of the Crocker
Laboratory 1.5 m cyclotron.
For bombardments of most isotopes, oxide targets of
0.1 to 0.5 mg/cm2 thickness were prepared by
electroplating from 0.4 M ammonium oxalate solution
onto 1 cm2 aluminum planchets. Foils of 0.002 cm
thickness were used for 238U bombardments and some
235U bombardments. Isotopic purity was greater than
93% for all targets.
Aluminum and platinum foils placed over
targets to degrade beam energy.
Converted from data set P0113 and updated.
BP: Updated entry, added doi and errors
information, digitized data (unobtainable data) per
an NRDC decision for data sets that were published
before 2000 (C32 of WP2022-01).
Random errors for the cross sections,
derived from estimated uncertainties in target
thickness, integrated beam current, chemical yield
and counting rate.
Table I, page 404 of J,NP,81,401,1966.

and counting rate.

sections, derived from estimated uncertainties in
target thickness, integrated beam current, chemical
yield and counting rate, are approximately +-20% for
spallation products and +-30% for fission products.
